FORTUNE it to track everything from pumas in Chile to chim- panzees in Rwandan rain- forests. Mobile-ready and device-agnostic, it enables easy data sharing to bolster collaboration. EarthRanger is also free—but its potential economic impact is vast. It has become a tool argu- ably indispensable to the global ecotourism market. And in Africa—where recent conservation efforts have helped halt steep declines in the numbers of elephants and rhinocer- oses—its value proposition is particularly striking.
